purpose: The commit changes the Bluetooth HCI sync queue logic so that if the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error instead of queuing commands. This prevents a use‑after‑free that could occur after the device is unregistered.
The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h from n_hdlc.c, relying on linux/tty.h which already pulls in linux/termios.h. This cleanup eliminates a redundant header dependency.
The commit updates the documentation for the 'N/A' store metric in the perf c2c report table, reflecting that this metric has been added for store operations.

This commit fixes several bugs in the hugetlb subsystem related to VMA lock handling. It resolves a race in hugetlb_unmap_file_folio when locks are dropped and reacquired, prevents double freeing of VMA locks during vma splitting by allocating a new lock for duplicated VMAs, and ensures that all VM_MAYSHARE VMA locks exist to avoid missing pmd sharing checks. Additionally, it changes the unmap routine to free the VMA lock instead of clearing VM_MAYSHARE, thereby preventing future bugs related to pmd sharing.

The commit adds documentation for two power supply properties (AVDD and MICVDD) to the device tree binding of the Realtek rt5682s codec. These supplies are already referenced in an existing device tree source, but the binding file lacked explicit entries for them. The change updates the binding to include these properties, enabling proper power supply configuration via device tree.

The commit adds a missing iounmap() call in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function, correcting a resource leak that occurs when the probe's error handling path releases a resource that the remove function previously failed to free.
The commit removes an unused 'work' field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the mlx5e kTLS code path, simplifying the data structure and eliminating dead code.

The commit changes the type of the key_idx field in cfg80211_auth_request from unsigned to signed, allowing it to hold the value -1 when the key is NULL. This change is made to eliminate static analysis warnings (e.g., from smatch) and to correctly represent the intended range of -1..3.
The commit reorganizes the order of the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS entries in the RISC-V defconfig files to match the sorting used by savedefconfig, without altering any functional behavior.

Adds a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to determine if the MCP251XFD chip’s FIFOs are configured for CAN‑FD mode, and replaces all inline checks with calls to this function, improving code readability and maintainability.
The commit modifies the RTC self-test suite to skip tests when no RTC device is present, preventing spurious failures.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in ALSA echoaudio midi.c, eliminating a dead store warning and cleaning up the code.

The commit changes the kernel build configuration so that the rcuscale module can be built without automatically pulling in RCU Tasks. Previously, a CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y kernel would always enable RCU Tasks unless rcuscale was built, which was unnecessary and increased kernel size and tracing complexity. This patch decouples the two, allowing rcuscale to be enabled independently.
The commit removes a duplicated string "be split" from the KVM x86 MMU header file spte.h, cleaning up the code.

The commit reverts a previous change that populated the dcn315 clock table using dcfclk values. The original change caused an underflow when pmfw disabled dcfclk states, leading to a no‑read situation. By reverting the change, the commit restores the original behavior and prevents the underflow until a better solution is implemented.
The commit adds a new compatible string entry for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC (R9A07G054) in the Mali‑Bifrost GPU device‑tree bindings, enabling the kernel to recognize this SoC’s Mali‑G31 GPU.

The commit renames the struct `pcie_port` to `dw_pcie_rp` in the dwc PCIe core driver to align its naming with the existing "dw_" prefix used for other driver entities, improving consistency and readability.

This commit corrects a printf‑style format string in the AMD XGBE driver. The original code used the %hx specifier for a u16 value that is promoted to int by the variadic function, which is undefined behaviour. The patch changes the format to %x, matching the promoted type and eliminating the clang -Wformat warning.

The commit corrects a bug in the MIPS jump_label subsystem where the upper bound of a branch range was compared using an unsigned comparison. This caused negative offsets to incorrectly trigger a warning. The fix casts the upper bound to a signed long before comparison, ensuring proper signed comparison and eliminating the false warning.

The commit removes an unnecessary case (HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD) from the SetHwReg8188EU() function in the r8188eu driver. Since that case only forwarded the call to another function, the commit replaces the case with a direct call to that function, thereby simplifying the code and eliminating an unused HAL layer element.
The commit changes the visibility of certain symbols in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c from global to static, eliminating sparse warnings about undeclared symbols and improving code encapsulation.

The commit removes an incorrect interrupt flags cell from MDSS nodes in the device tree bindings. MDSS provides a single interrupt cell, so specifying flags on consumers is invalid. The change corrects the binding to prevent misconfiguration.

The commit adds allocation of array space in the struct ctrl_ref used by the V4L2 controls subsystem, enabling drivers to change the array size dynamically. This change introduces new functionality that was not previously available.

The commit extends the Qualcomm socinfo driver by adding entries for several new System-on-Chips (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, an additional SM8450) and associated PMICs. This enables the driver to recognize and report these devices on boards that use them.

The commit updates the rtl8xxxu Wi‑Fi driver to correctly recognize all chip cuts (letters A–P). Previously, any chip cut greater than E was reported as "unknown"; now the driver will handle all valid cuts.

The commit removes the custom .is_enabled field from the PRCMU clock structures in the ux500 clock driver. The Linux kernel clock core already tracks a software enable count, so this field is redundant. The change simply deletes the field and any associated logic, cleaning up the code.

The commit reorganizes the use of the CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL preprocessor guard in the RISC-V memory management initialization code. It moves the pt_ops macro to be adjacent to the guard and groups related declarations that were previously scattered, thereby reducing the number of #ifdef blocks. No new functionality is added and no existing bug is directly fixed.

The commit adds device‑tree entries for the QUP (Qualcomm Universal Peripheral) and GPI (Generic Peripheral Interface) DMA controllers on the SM6350‑Lena platform, enabling the kernel to use these peripherals for I2C and other functions.

The commit updates comments in the rzg2l thermal driver: it replaces a vague comment with a clearer one, corrects a typo in the word "calibration", and fixes a coding style issue by moving the opening comment delimiter to a separate line. No functional code changes are made.

This commit fixes an infinite loop in the igc driver’s release_swfw_sync routine. The loop occurs when the driver cannot acquire the hardware semaphore, typically after a surprise hardware removal. The patch replaces the loop with error logging and a graceful exit, preventing the driver from hanging.
The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace legacy bus clock defines with modern ASoC DAI format terminology, improving code clarity and aligning with current API conventions.

The commit removes a redundant preprocessor check for the macro IS_SWAPFILE in the NFS subsystem. Since IS_SWAPFILE is always defined, the check serves no purpose and is eliminated to simplify the code.
Adds kernel-side handling of GTP echo requests by sending echo responses with the required recovery IE and restart counter, thereby fulfilling the GTP specification.

The commit replaces calls to cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* where the logic is trivial, and introduces a new helper cpumask_first_zero() to replace the missing cpumask_first_zero() functionality. This change improves performance and code clarity by using the more efficient first‑based functions in appropriate contexts.

The commit removes a call to the firmware IOVAR "dtim_assoc" in the brcmfmac driver. The call was always failing because the firmware no longer supports it, causing repeated error messages during STA roaming. By eliminating the call, the driver no longer attempts the unsupported operation, eliminating the console spam and correcting the driver’s behavior.

Adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for the Intel ASoC driver to support the alc5682 headset codec on platforms that lack a speaker amplifier, wiring the codec to SSP0 following the Intel Bluetooth offload design.

This commit adds a check in the io_uring/net code to make zerocopy sendmsg fail when the underlying socket does not support zerocopy. It mirrors a previous change that handled zerocopy send requests, ensuring that unsupported protocols correctly return an error instead of silently succeeding.

The commit modifies the kernel documentation preamble to add a check for the presence of the xeCJK.sty file before attempting to load it. This prevents a build error on systems where the texlive-xecjk package is installed but the xeCJK.sty file is missing, such as on RHEL 9. The change addresses an existing bug that caused "xeCJK.sty not found!" errors during PDF documentation generation.

The commit modifies the checkpatch script to suppress false‑positive warnings for macros that expand to "case" statements, which are already used in some in‑kernel drivers. It changes the linting rule that requires complex macro values to be parenthesized, allowing these specific macros to pass without error.

The commit replaces the simple-KMS helper functions in the simple DRM driver with the standard atomic helpers. This refactoring removes empty CRTC helper functions and ensures that plane state checks are always performed. No functional behavior changes are introduced; the change is purely architectural to improve code sharing and maintainability.

This commit introduces a new JPEG decoding worker interface in the MediaTek JPEG decoder driver. The worker allows three hardware decoders to operate concurrently on the MT8195 platform, enabling parallel processing of JPEG streams.

This commit removes VPRINTK() debug prints and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper from libata, as the information is already available via tracepoints or SCSI tracing, making the code cleaner and reducing unnecessary output.

The commit adds support for entering zstate during the extended vblank period that occurs when the display enters FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. It calculates the extra time available from the added vblank lines, allows zstate entry only if that time exceeds 5 ms, writes an optimized value for min_dst_y_next_start to a register, and restores defaults when exiting freesync.

The commit changes the prototype of the hqd_destroy() callback in the AMD KFD DRM driver so that its third parameter type matches the actual type used by all implementations (enum kfd_preempt_type). This resolves a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure that occurs when the kernel is compiled with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG on certain GPUs.

The commit corrects the property name in the STM32 pinctrl device tree binding from the incorrect 'reset' to the proper 'resets', and adds the missing 'additionalProperties: false' to the binding schema. This fixes a binding bug that could cause misinterpretation of device tree nodes and strengthens validation to prevent future misconfigurations.

The commit adds a suppression for a GCC 12 array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver, specifically in the ath9k_hw_resettxqueue function. It does not change functional behavior but removes a compiler warning that the author could not resolve.

The commit replaces calls to pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver. This change uses a more suitable runtime PM API, simplifying the code path and ensuring the device is properly resumed before use.

The commit reorganizes io_uring code by moving the fixed file installation and removal logic into the filetable module, and changes helper functions to accept a context instead of an io_kiocb. It is a refactoring that improves code structure and clarity.

The commit updates the device tree bindings for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU to split the previously combined G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes. This change aligns the bindings with the hardware’s power‑domain controller (vpu‑blk‑ctrl) and allows each decoder to be enabled independently. The update introduces new binding definitions that enable separate configuration of G1 and G2, which was not previously supported.

The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro, which was only used once inside the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, and inlines its contents directly into IWL_DEVICE_BZ. This simplifies the code by eliminating an unnecessary macro.
